lint_issues.message: Multiple imports on one line: sqlite3, pickle, subprocess
lint_issues.suggestion: Split into separate lines: import sqlite3 / import pickle / import subprocess

lint_issues.message: Hardcoded database password detected in variable 'DB_PASSWORD'
lint_issues.suggestion: Use environment variables (os.getenv) or a .env config file instead of hardcoding secrets

lint_issues.message: SQL injection vulnerability: f-string interpolates user input directly into SQL query
lint_issues.suggestion: Use parameterized queries: conn.execute('SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = ?', (username,))

lint_issues.message: Insecure deserialization via pickle.load() on untrusted data
lint_issues.suggestion: Use a safe serialization format like JSON. If pickle is required, validate the source

lint_issues.message: Multiple statements on one line: 'with' block body on same line as statement
lint_issues.suggestion: Move 'return pickle.load(f)' to its own indented line below the 'with' statement

lint_issues.message: Command injection vulnerability: subprocess.call with shell=True and f-string interpolating user input
lint_issues.suggestion: Use subprocess.run([cmd]) without shell=True, or pass arguments as a list

lint_issues.message: Multiple statements on one line: function body on same line as def statement
lint_issues.suggestion: Move 'return a / b' to its own indented line below 'def divide(a, b):'

structure_issues.message: Variable 'DB_PASSWORD' is defined but never referenced in any function body
structure_issues.suggestion: Remove unused variable or integrate it into database connection logic

structure_issues.message: Function 'divide' has no zero-division guard; division by zero will raise ZeroDivisionError
structure_issues.suggestion: Add a check: if b == 0: return None or raise ValueError('Cannot divide by zero')
